content.problemStatement:
"All the pain in the world will not help you find something that does not exist..."

You are Dastan, the great Prince of Persia!

Under the tunnels of Alamut, you have discovered a path вАХ a sequence of NNN tiles (numbered 111 through NNN) ahead of you. You are currently on tile 111. There is a signboard beside you, which says thus:


Stepping on tile iii (for each valid iii) activates a switch described by a binary string SiS_iSiвАЛ with length NNN. When you fell through the tunnels onto tile 111, switch S1S_1S1вАЛ got activated.
For each valid iii, when the switch SiS_iSiвАЛ gets activated, then for each jjj (1вЙ§jвЙ§N)1 \le j \le N)1вЙ§jвЙ§N), if the jjj-th character of SiS_iSiвАЛ is '0', tile jjj becomes unsafe to step on; otherwise (if the jjj-th character is '1'), tile jjj becomes safe to step on.
Step on an unsafe tile and you shall testify the fate of all men вАХ death!
You shall find that which you seek on reaching tile NNN. This tile must also be safe when you step on it.
Being a trained warrior, you are able to make jumps with length up to KKK tiles, **in both directions**. In other words, if you are currently at a tile iii, you may jump to a tile jjj if jjj is a valid tile and вИ£iвИТjвИ£вЙ§K|i-j| \le KвИ£iвИТjвИ£вЙ§K.

You want to reach tile NNN as quickly as possible вАХ you want to minimise the number of jumps you need to make. Find this minimum number of jumps.
constraints: S1,S2,вА¶,SNS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_NS1вАЛ,S2вАЛ,вА¶,SNвАЛ contain only characters '0' and '1'
for each valid iii, the iii-th digit of SiS_iSiвАЛ is '1'
the sum of N2N^2N2 over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
NNN lines follow. For each iii (1вЙ§iвЙ§N1 \le i \le N1вЙ§iвЙ§N), the iii-th of these lines contains a single string SiS_iSiвАЛ with length NNN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer вАХ the minimum number of jumps you need to reach your target, or вИТ1-1вИТ1 if it is impossible.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The optimal sequence of jumps is 1вЖТ2вЖТ4вЖТ3вЖТ51 \rightarrow 2 \rightarrow 4 \rightarrow 3 \rightarrow 51вЖТ2вЖТ4вЖТ3вЖТ5. A shorter sequence does not exist.
Example case 2: There is no sequence of jumps that would allow you to reach tile 333.
